articles.title: A $2,000 AI-generated film will make its debut at Tribeca
articles.description: Next month's Tribeca Festival will include the premiere of an AI-generated film: Dreams of Violets. The 75-minute film is a fictional dramatization of the Iranian government's mass killing of protestors in January, with the people and images fully created by AI, as reported earlier by The Hollywood
articles.content: Next month's Tribeca Festival will include the premiere of an AI-generated film: Dreams of Violets . The 75-minute film is a fictional dramatization of the Iranian government's mass killing of protestors in January, with the people and images fully created by AI, as reported earlier by The Hollywood Reporter . Dreams of Violets cost $2,000 to make and is "based on journalistic reports, photographs, and eyewitness accounts," according to a press release . It was created by Ash and Pooya Koosha, t
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.content: Anthropic is releasing Claude Opus 4.8 on Thursday, and the company is touting the model's "honesty." According to Anthropic , it trains "all [its] models to be honest - for instance, to avoid making claims that they can't support." But it notes that "a general problem with AI models is that they sometimes jump to conclusions, confidently presenting their work as making progress despite thin evidence." The AI lab claims that early testers have found that Opus 4.8 "is more likely to flag uncertai

articles.content: Researchers uncover how popular parameter-efficient finetuning techniques balance learning new tasks against forgetting existing capabilities. articles.title: Steam Deck sells out in North America within 24 hours of price hike
articles.description: Europe, Australia, and Asia can still get it while the getting's good.
articles.content: Well, that was fast. Less than 24 hours after Valve announced renewed availability of the Steam Deck OLED (at a massively increased MSRP), the handheld is once again listed as "out of stock" in the US and Canada. Spot checks of other regional Steam stores on Thursday morning showed the hardware as still available across Europe and Australia for the time being, as well as in Asian countries through Valve's sales partner Komodo. While it's hard to know from the outside just how many Steam Deck uni

articles.content: Stop Reinventing the Wheel: 5 Hidden Gems in PrestaShopтАЩs Tools.php File ЁЯза Introduction: The Modern DeveloperтАЩs Paradox ThereтАЩs a tension I often feel when talking with developers coming from the pure Symfony universe. articles.description: Activision is planning to drop support for PS4 and Xbox One consoles in Call of Duty: Warzone later this year. Players will need to upgrade to a PS5 or Xbox Series S / X console to continue playing Call of Duty: Warzone once season 6 of Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 finishes later this […]
articles.content: Activision is planning to drop support for PS4 and Xbox One consoles in Call of Duty: Warzone later this year. Players will need to upgrade to a PS5 or Xbox Series S / X console to continue playing Call of Duty: Warzone once season 6 of Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 finishes later this year. Call of Duty: Warzone will be removed from PS4 and Xbox One storefronts on June 4th and will no longer be available to download. Activision will then remove the in-game store in Warzone on PS4 and Xbox One on Ju
articles.author: Tom Warren

Climate tech companies are going public. WhatтАЩs next? Solar and battery company Solv Energy went public in February, hitting a $6 billion valuation. X
articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Climate tech companies are going public. WhatтАЩs next? Solar and battery company Solv Energy went public in February, hitting a $6 billion valuation. X-energy, which builds small modular nuclear reactors, followed at $11.5 billion. articles.content: It is one thing to say AI will change the world. It is another to expect the class of 2026 to applaud it. In fact, when former Google CEO Eric Schmidt told University of Arizona graduates that their task is to help shape AI, he was met with a resounding chorus of boos. тАЬI can hear you,тАЭ he said, before conceding that fears about disappearing jobs and a broken future were тАЬrational.тАЭ This is not exactly the message one hopes to hear while sweating under a polyester gown and tallying student loan

articles.title: Forecasters predict below-average hurricane season, advise against complacency
articles.description: Forecasters say expected El Ni├▒o should temper hurricanes in Atlantic, urge preparedness.
articles.content: Forecasters are calling for below-average activity this hurricane season, which begins Monday, June 1. The National Weather Service is predicting eight to 14 named storms, including three to six hurricanes and one to three major hurricanes of category 3, 4, or 5 strength, packing winds of 111 mph or greater. By comparison, a typical season is characterized by 14 named storms, including seven hurricanes and three major hurricanes. The season ends November 30. тАЬIt just takes one,тАЭ said Ken Graham,
articles.author: Amy Green

articles.title: Climate tech companies are going public. WhatтАЩs next?
articles.description: This year, thereтАЩs been a wave of notable energy companies going public via IPO in the US. The solar and battery company Solv Energy went public in February, to the tune of $6 billion. X-energy, which is building small modular nuclear reactors, did the same in April, and its stocks surged on its fir
articles.content: This year, thereтАЩs been a wave of notable energy companies going public via IPO in the US. The solar and battery company Solv Energy went public in February, to the tune of $6 billion. X-energy, which is building small modular nuclear reactors, did the same in April, and its stocks surged on its first day of trading to hit a $11.5 billion market cap. Most recently, the geothermal company Fervo Energy went public in mid-May, and its market cap is now about $12.4 billion . Those are all success st
articles.author: Casey Crownhart

articles.title: Bad cholesterol slashed 62% by single dose of gene-editing drug in small trial
articles.description: The interim Phase I trial data was only from 35 people, but results look good so far.
articles.content: An experimental gene-editing therapy that aims to lower bad cholesterol for the long-term after a single infusion is off to a positive start in an early clinical trial. Researchers running a Phase I safety trial for the drug, dubbed VERVE-102, published interim results from just 35 patients this week in the New England Journal of Medicine . Though the numbers are small and the analysis is preliminary, VERVE-102 appeared safe, with no serious adverse events reported from the treatment, even at th
articles.author: Beth Mole
